title: Six Iranian-linked tankers cluster southeast of Hormuz for probable ship-to-ship transfers
summary: Satellite data from Windward and Kpler reveals a cluster of six Iranian-linked tankers loitering 200nm southeast of the Strait of Hormuz with AIS transponders disabled. Synthetic Aperture Radar imagery confirms transfer activity.
contents:
# Six Iranian-Linked Tankers Detected in Probable STS Transfer Zone

## Detection
Windward and Kpler satellite monitoring detected a cluster of six tankers, including the Iranian-linked vessel Riah (high VRMS score), loitering approximately 200 nautical miles southeast of the Strait of Hormuz. All vessels disabled AIS transponders during the activity period.

## Evidence
- Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery confirms ship-to-ship transfer operations
- AIS transponders remain off across all six vessels
- VRMS (Vessel Risk Management Scores) flag elevated sanctions evasion risk
- Activity pattern matches known Iranian crude export evasion tactics

## Context
This cluster emerged amid the ongoing US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian President Pezeshkian called the blockade "intolerable" as oil prices surge past $125 per barrel. Iran continues leveraging dark fleet operations to circumvent sanctions and maintain crude exports, primarily to Chinese buyers.

## Historical Pattern
TankerTrackers daily briefs (April 30) noted a 15% year-over-year increase in sanctioned oil shipments via Kpler analytics. The US State Department sanctioned 12 additional vessels in April 2026.

## Tags
tanker-tracking, iran, sanctions-evasion, ship-to-ship-transfer, dark-fleet, strait-of-hormuz, oil
